I'm perplexed as to why the app doesn't provide a straightforward way to adjust the vocal prominence in favor of instrumental layers, allowing me to savor the nuances of the composition sans overpowering harmonies.

While both Apple Music and Spotify have their strengths, one feature that sets Spotify apart is its seamless radio creation experience. When I'm in the mood to discover new music, Spotify's instant download option for radio playlists is a game-changer - it allows me to quickly add tracks to my library without having to manually download each song individually, as is the case with Apple Music. This streamlined process saves me time and effort, making Spotify the clear winner when it comes to curating personalized playlists on the fly
